task_completion.description: Navigate to the Golden Gate Bridge area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main 3D viewport shows the Golden Gate Bridge.
A dialog box titled 'New Placemark' or 'Google Earth - New Placemark' (or 'Properties') is visible.
The 'Name' field in the dialog contains the text 'Golden Gate Hero Shot'.
The 'Description' field in the dialog contains the text 'Marketing viewpoint - Marin Headlands perspective'.

The view shows the bridge from the North/Marin side looking South-West (approx 220°) with an oblique angle (not top-down), OR the 'View' tab in the dialog is visible showing Range ~1500, Tilt ~65, Heading ~220.

task_completion.description: Save the placemark to the Places list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The placemark 'Golden Gate Hero Shot' appears in the 'Places' or 'My Places' sidebar panel.

integrity.description: Verify agent uses the application GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Google Earth interface (menus, dialogs, map controls) rather than a command line or script.
The agent is seen typing or pasting text into the dialog fields within the application, not executing a bulk import command.
The map view shows a rendered 3D scene of the bridge, not a static image or external photo loaded into the viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Golden Gate Bridge is located at 37.8199°N, -122.4783°W. A heading of 220° corresponds to a South-West direction. The Marin Headlands are located to the North of the bridge.
